articles.headline: Commemorating the Dandi March: A Symbol of Non-Violent Resistance and Economic Self-Reliance
articles.explainer:
• April 6 marks the anniversary of the conclusion of Mahatma Gandhi's historic Dandi March in 1930, a pivotal moment in India's freedom struggle.
• The Salt Satyagraha challenged the British salt monopoly, symbolizing economic exploitation and colonial oppression.
• It mobilized widespread civil disobedience, drawing millions into the non-violent independence movement.
• The event underscored the power of non-violent resistance (Satyagraha) and the pursuit of Swaraj (self-rule) and economic self-sufficiency.
prelimsPoints: Dandi March began on March 12, 1930, from Sabarmati Ashram.
Concluded on April 6, 1930, at Dandi, breaking the salt law.
Led by Mahatma Gandhi with 78 initial followers.
Part of the wider Civil Disobedience Movement.
Lord Irwin was the Viceroy of India during this period.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Dandi March as a watershed moment in India's freedom struggle, highlighting its impact on mass mobilization.
Analyze how the Salt Satyagraha effectively challenged British economic policies and colonial legitimacy.
Examine the enduring relevance of Gandhian principles of non-violence and self-reliance demonstrated by the Dandi March in contemporary governance and social movements.
Evaluate the role of women and various social groups in the Civil Disobedience Movement initiated by the Dandi March.
articles.imageDescription: Mahatma Gandhi leading the Dandi March with his followers.
articles.trivia: The distance covered during the Dandi March was approximately 385 kilometers (240 miles) over 24 days.

articles.headline: Bharatiya Janata Party Marks 45th Foundation Day, Reflects on Political Journey
articles.explainer:
• April 6, 2025, marks the 45th Foundation Day of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), established in 1980.
• The party emerged from the erstwhile Jana Sangh, which was a constituent of the Janata Party.
• Its core ideology revolves around 'Integral Humanism' and 'cultural nationalism' (Hindutva).
• Over decades, the BJP has grown from a marginal political force to the dominant party in Indian politics, leading multiple coalition and majority governments.
prelimsPoints: BJP was founded on April 6, 1980.
Its predecessor was the Bharatiya Jana Sangh, founded in 1951 by Syama Prasad Mookerjee.
Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the first President of the BJP.
The party's symbol is the lotus.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Bharatiya Janata Party since its inception, tracing its ideological shifts and electoral strategies.
Discuss the impact of 'cultural nationalism' on India's political landscape, with specific reference to the BJP's rise.
Evaluate the role of the BJP in shaping India's socio-economic policies over the past few decades.
articles.imageDescription: BJP party symbol (Lotus) and a group of leaders.
articles.trivia: The BJP's first major electoral breakthrough came in 1989, securing 85 seats in the Lok Sabha.

articles.headline: Roman Catholic Prelate Paschal Topno Passes Away at 92
articles.explainer:
• Most Rev. Paschal Topno, a prominent Roman Catholic prelate, passed away at the age of 92.
• He served as the Archbishop of Ranchi, a significant diocese in India.
• His life was dedicated to spiritual guidance and social work, particularly in tribal regions of Jharkhand.
• His contributions included promoting education and healthcare in underserved communities.
prelimsPoints: Paschal Topno was the Archbishop of Ranchi.
He served the Roman Catholic Church in India.
articles.trivia: Ranchi diocese holds historical significance for its early missionary work among tribal communities in central India.

articles.headline: Indian Hockey Legend Raghbir Lal Breathes His Last at 95
articles.explainer:
• Raghbir Lal, a two-time Olympic gold medalist in hockey, passed away at the age of 95.
• He was a pivotal member of the Indian hockey teams that won gold medals at the 1952 Helsinki and 1956 Melbourne Olympics.
• His demise marks the loss of a significant figure from India's golden era of hockey.
• He inspired generations of athletes with his dedication and skill on the field.
prelimsPoints: Raghbir Lal was an Indian field hockey player.
He won Olympic gold medals in 1952 (Helsinki) and 1956 (Melbourne).
India dominated field hockey during his playing career.
articles.imageDescription: Vintage photo of Raghbir Lal playing hockey.
articles.trivia: India won eight Olympic gold medals in men's field hockey between 1928 and 1980, a world record.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Kishna Ram Nai Passes Away at 90
articles.explainer:
• Kishna Ram Nai, a veteran politician known for his contributions to regional politics, passed away at the age of 90.
• He served multiple terms as a legislator, advocating for the welfare of his constituents.
• His career spanned several decades, witnessing significant changes in India's political landscape.
• Nai was respected across party lines for his commitment to public service.
prelimsPoints: Kishna Ram Nai was a veteran Indian politician.
He served as a legislator for multiple terms.
articles.imageDescription: Photo of Kishna Ram Nai addressing a gathering.
articles.trivia: The age of eligibility for becoming a member of a State Legislative Assembly (MLA) in India is 25 years.

articles.headline: UN Observes International Day of Sport for Development and Peace, India's Role Highlighted
articles.explainer:
• April 6 is recognized as the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace by the United Nations.
• The day highlights the potential of sport to foster peace, promote social inclusion, and achieve sustainable development goals.
• India actively participates in global initiatives utilizing sport for youth development and community building.
• Discussions focus on leveraging sports programs for health, education, and gender equality.
prelimsPoints: International Day of Sport for Development and Peace is observed annually on April 6.
It was proclaimed by the UN General Assembly in 2013.
The day aims to recognize the positive role of sport in society.
mainsPoints: Discuss how sports can be an effective tool for achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in India, particularly in areas like health, education, and gender equality.
Analyze India's current policies and initiatives aimed at promoting sports for development and peace, and suggest areas for improvement.
Examine the role of international cooperation and UN initiatives in fostering a culture of peace and development through sports globally.
articles.imageDescription: People participating in various sports activities, symbolizing unity and peace.
articles.trivia: The white flag, representing peace and neutrality, has been a symbol of the Olympic movement since 1920.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Green Hydrogen Production
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government has announced a comprehensive policy framework to boost green hydrogen production and utilization.
• The policy aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen and contribute to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key provisions include incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers, promoting R&D, and establishing green hydrogen corridors.
• This initiative is crucial for India's commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070 and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims to become a major producer and exporter of green hydrogen.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched in 2023.
Applications include fertilizers, refineries, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the new Green Hydrogen Policy in achieving India's energy security and climate change mitigation targets.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India, including technological, economic, and infrastructural aspects.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform key industrial sectors and contribute to job creation and economic growth.
Compare India's green hydrogen strategy with global efforts and its potential to establish global leadership in this sector.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Evolving Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to balance controlling inflation with supporting economic growth.
• Global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility continue to pose upside risks to inflation.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while remaining watchful of growth dynamics.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rates.
The repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
The MPC consists of six members: three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate, considering both domestic and global economic factors.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting sustainable economic growth in the current macroeconomic environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in influencing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with a graph showing economic indicators.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Tiger Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• India has reaffirmed its commitment to global tiger conservation, pledging increased financial and technical support.
• This comes as the latest tiger census indicates a stable to rising population in Indian reserves.
• The focus will be on combating poaching, habitat restoration, and transboundary cooperation.
• India, home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, plays a crucial role in the species' survival.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in India in 1973.
India has the largest tiger population globally.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) oversees Project Tiger.
Global Tiger Day is celebrated on July 29.
mainsPoints: Discuss the successes and challenges of India's tiger conservation efforts since the launch of Project Tiger.
Analyze the ecological and economic benefits of tiger conservation, including its role as an umbrella species.
Examine the strategies for mitigating human-wildlife conflict in areas surrounding tiger reserves.
Evaluate the importance of international cooperation in transboundary tiger conservation landscapes.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing conservation.
articles.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals and typically hunt at night.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has conducted a successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing cost-effective and sustainable space access for India.
• The RLV program aims to reduce launch costs and enhance payload capacity by reusing rocket stages.
• India is among a few nations developing this advanced space technology, bolstering its position in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Achievements in Science and Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) is a crucial step towards future RLV.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
Other countries like the USA (SpaceX) are also developing similar technologies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its implications for global space competition.
Analyze the economic and technological advantages of RLVs compared to traditional expendable launch vehicles.
Examine the potential applications of advanced space technologies like RLVs in areas such as remote sensing, communication, and national security.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle during re-entry.
articles.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was the first interplanetary mission to be successfully launched by India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act Amendments
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India is scheduled to hear multiple petitions challenging recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners raise concerns regarding potential infringements on privacy rights, government access to data, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India, balancing individual rights with the need for data processing.
• The outcome will have significant implications for data privacy, fundamental rights, and the digital economy in India.
articles.source: Supreme Court of India / Legal News
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to regulate digital personal data.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
The Data Protection Board of India is established under the Act.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and certain processing outside India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in light of the Right to Privacy as enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
Discuss the potential impact of the DPDP Act on individual privacy, government surveillance, and the ease of doing business in the digital economy.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights in the context of evolving digital laws and technologies.
Suggest measures to strengthen data protection mechanisms while balancing national security and economic interests.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution protects the right to life and personal liberty, which the Supreme Court has interpreted to include the right to privacy.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Defence Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and defence technology.
• Discussions covered joint maritime security initiatives, counter-terrorism efforts, and strategic autonomy in a multipolar world.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, countering growing regional challenges.
• Defence collaboration, including technology transfer and co-production, remains a cornerstone of the bilateral relationship.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership.
France is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region.
Defence deals, like the Rafale fighter jets, are significant aspects of their cooperation.
Both nations advocate for a rules-based international order.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic partnership between India and France, particularly its significance in the context of Indo-Pacific geopolitics.
Discuss the various pillars of India-France cooperation, focusing on defence, space, and emerging technologies.
Evaluate how the strategic autonomy vision of both nations converges and diverges in shaping their foreign policy approaches.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India-France collaboration in addressing regional and global security threats.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of 'PM-Gati Shakti' Master Plan for Multi-Modal Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The government conducted a high-level review of the 'PM-Gati Shakti' National Master Plan, assessing its progress in enhancing multi-modal infrastructure connectivity.
• The plan aims to integrate 16 ministries for holistic planning and synchronized implementation of infrastructure projects.
• It utilizes technology, including GIS-based mapping, to reduce logistics costs, improve efficiency, and boost economic growth.
• The review highlighted advancements in various sectors like roads, railways, ports, and digital infrastructure.
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.
It integrates 16 ministries including Railways, Roads, Ports, Shipping, Aviation, Petroleum, Power, Telecom.
The plan aims to reduce logistics costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
It uses a GIS-based platform for synchronized project planning and monitoring.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of the 'PM-Gati Shakti' National Master Plan and its potential to transform India's infrastructure landscape.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a large-scale, multi-ministerial integrated planning initiative and suggest ways to overcome them.
Evaluate the expected economic benefits of improved multi-modal connectivity, including reduction in logistics costs and enhanced industrial competitiveness.
Examine how PM-Gati Shakti can contribute to India's goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub.
articles.imageDescription: Digital map showing integrated infrastructure projects under PM-Gati Shakti.
articles.trivia: India's logistics costs are estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than global benchmarks, which PM Gati Shakti aims to reduce.

articles.headline: Historical Significance of Sabarmati Ashram in India's Freedom Struggle
articles.explainer:
• Sabarmati Ashram, located in Ahmedabad, served as one of Mahatma Gandhi's primary residences and a center for his activities from 1917 to 1930.
• It was from here that Gandhi launched the Dandi March on March 12, 1930, marking the beginning of the Salt Satyagraha.
• The Ashram was a laboratory for Gandhi's experiments with truth (Satyagraha), self-sufficiency (Swadeshi), and social reform.
• It remains a significant historical site, preserving Gandhi's legacy and inspiring principles of non-violence and simple living.
articles.source: Sabarmati Ashram Preservation and Memorial Trust
prelimsPoints: Sabarmati Ashram is located on the banks of the Sabarmati River in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.
Gandhi lived here from 1917 to 1930.
It was originally known as Satyagraha Ashram.
The Dandi March began from this Ashram.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted role of Sabarmati Ashram in shaping Mahatma Gandhi's philosophy and its contribution to the Indian freedom struggle.
Analyze how the Ashram served as a model for community living and a training ground for non-violent resistance.
Examine the enduring relevance of the ideals propagated at Sabarmati Ashram in contemporary India, particularly concerning self-reliance and social justice.
articles.imageDescription: Mahatma Gandhi's 'Hriday Kunj' at Sabarmati Ashram.
articles.trivia: Gandhi vowed not to return to the Sabarmati Ashram until India achieved independence.

articles.headline: Rising Concerns Over Microplastic Pollution in Indian Rivers and Ecosystems
articles.explainer:
• Recent studies indicate an alarming increase in microplastic pollution across major Indian rivers, posing significant environmental and health risks.
• Microplastics, tiny plastic particles, enter river systems from various sources including industrial waste, sewage, and improper plastic disposal.
• Their presence impacts aquatic life, enters the food chain, and has potential long-term effects on human health.
• Experts call for urgent policy interventions, improved waste management, and public awareness campaigns to tackle this growing crisis.
prelimsPoints: Microplastics are plastic particles less than 5mm in size.
They originate from larger plastic debris breakdown and microbeads in products.
Microplastics can carry toxins and enter the food web.
The Ganga River is one of the most studied rivers for microplastic pollution in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the sources and pathways of microplastic pollution in Indian river systems and their ecological consequences.
Discuss the potential health risks associated with microplastic contamination in water and food, and the need for comprehensive research.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy involving policy, technology, and community participation to mitigate microplastic pollution in India.
Examine the role of a circular economy approach and improved waste management practices in addressing plastic pollution.
articles.imageDescription: Microplastic particles visible in water, with a river in the background.
articles.trivia: A single plastic bottle can take up to 450 years to decompose in the environment.

articles.headline: Digital Payments Witness Sustained Growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities, Driving Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• Digital payment transactions in India continue to surge, with significant adoption observed in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities and rural areas.
• Government initiatives like UPI (Unified Payments Interface) and Jan Dhan accounts have democratized access to digital financial services.
• This growth is fostering financial inclusion, enabling easier access to credit, and boosting local economies.
• Challenges remain in terms of digital literacy, infrastructure, and cybersecurity, requiring continued policy focus.
articles.source: National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) / Economic Survey
prelimsPoints: UPI is developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) aimed at financial inclusion.
India is a global leader in real-time digital payments.
The number of active internet users in rural areas is growing rapidly.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of digital payments, particularly UPI, in driving financial inclusion and empowering rural economies in India.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges associated with the rapid expansion of digital financial services in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Examine the government's initiatives and policy measures to promote digital payments and address issues like digital divide and cybersecurity risks.
Evaluate the potential of fintech innovations to further deepen financial inclusion and economic growth across India.
articles.imageDescription: People using mobile phones for digital payments in a market setting.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 13.4 billion transactions worth ₹19.78 lakh crore in March 2024, reflecting its immense popularity.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Annual Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Climate Concerns
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting to discuss issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The dialogue focused on sharing data, resolving minor disputes, and the impact of climate change on water flows.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of six rivers between the two countries.
• Despite political tensions, the treaty has largely endured as a model of water-sharing cooperation.
articles.source: Ministry of Water Resources / External Affairs
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960.
It was brokered by the World Bank.
India has control over the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).
Pakistan has control over the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
mainsPoints: Analyze the enduring significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a framework for water-sharing between India and Pakistan despite political challenges.
Discuss the potential impacts of climate change on the Indus Basin and the need for enhanced cooperation between riparian states.
Examine the role of international mediation in resolving transboundary water disputes and the lessons learned from the IWT.
Evaluate India's rights and responsibilities under the IWT concerning hydropower projects on the Western Rivers.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world.

highlights: India Commemorates Dandi March Anniversary, Highlighting Non-Violent Resistance and Self-Reliance.
BJP Marks 45th Foundation Day, Reflecting on its Political Journey and Ideological Evolution.
Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Boost Green Hydrogen Production, Targeting Energy Security.
ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology, Advancing Cost-Effective Space Access.
Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act Amendments, Raising Privacy Concerns.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Dandi March (1930), consider the following statements:
1. It was launched from Sabarmati Ashram and concluded at Dandi.
2. Mahatma Gandhi was accompanied by over a thousand followers throughout the march.
3. The primary objective was to protest against the British textile monopoly.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Dandi March began from Sabarmati Ashram on March 12, 1930, and concluded at Dandi on April 6, 1930. Statement 2 is incorrect; Gandhi started with 78 trusted followers, though thousands joined along the way. Statement 3 is incorrect; the primary objective was to protest against the British salt monopoly, not the textile monopoly.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is/are correct?
1. It was founded on April 6, 1980.
2. Its ideological predecessor was the Swatantra Party.
3. Atal Bihari Vajpayee was its first President.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, BJP was founded on April 6, 1980. Statement 2 is incorrect; its ideological predecessor was the Bharatiya Jana Sangh. Statement 3 is correct, Atal Bihari Vajpayee was indeed the first President of the BJP.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Green Hydrogen:
1. It is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
2. India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
3. The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched in 2015.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Green hydrogen is produced through electrolysis powered by renewable energy. Statement 2 is correct, India has ambitious plans to lead in green hydrogen. Statement 3 is incorrect; the National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched in 2023, not 2015.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan with the mediation of which of the following organizations?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governing the sharing of six rivers between India and Pakistan.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of the 'PM-Gati Shakti' National Master Plan?
options: (a) To promote sustainable agriculture in drought-prone regions.
(b) To integrate infrastructure planning and implementation across multiple ministries.
(c) To provide universal healthcare coverage to all citizens.
(d) To establish new smart cities across the country.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To integrate infrastructure planning and implementation across multiple ministries.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PM-Gati Shakti National Master Plan aims to integrate 16 ministries for holistic planning and synchronized implementation of infrastructure projects, thereby reducing logistics costs and improving efficiency.

mainsMocks.question: The Dandi March was not merely a symbolic act but a strategic masterpiece that galvanized the Indian masses. Discuss how the Salt Satyagraha effectively challenged colonial authority and laid the groundwork for future mass movements. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Modern Indian History - The Freedom Struggle
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Dandi March as a pivotal event. Discuss its symbolism (salt tax, economic exploitation) and strategic aspects (mass mobilization, non-violence). Explain how it undermined British legitimacy and inspired wider civil disobedience. Conclude with its legacy for the freedom struggle.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the potential of Green Hydrogen as a cornerstone of India's energy transition and its implications for achieving net-zero emission targets. What are the key challenges in scaling up its production and adoption in India? (250 words)
mainsMocks.approach: Define Green Hydrogen and its importance for energy security and decarbonization. Discuss its potential applications and benefits (reducing imports, new industry). Identify challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, technological maturity, and policy support. Suggest measures to overcome these.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate the provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in the context of the Right to Privacy in India. How does the Act balance individual data rights with governmental and economic interests, and what are the concerns raised by its implementation? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution - Fundamental Rights, Government Policies and Interventions
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act and the constitutional Right to Privacy (Puttaswamy judgment). Explain key provisions like data fiduciary, data principal rights, and exceptions. Analyze how it attempts to balance privacy with state/economic needs. Discuss concerns regarding government access, Data Protection Board's independence, and potential impact on fundamental rights and surveillance.

quickBytes.text: April 6, 1930, marked the end of the Dandi March, launching the Civil Disobedience Movement.
The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) was founded on April 6, 1980.
International Day of Sport for Development and Peace is observed annually on April 6.
Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy, aiming for net-zero.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's policy interest rates, including the repo rate.
Project Tiger was launched in India in 1973 to protect the national animal.
ISRO is actively developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for cost-effective space access.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, regulates digital personal data in India.
PM Gati Shakti Master Plan integrates 16 ministries for multi-modal infrastructure development.
The Indus Waters Treaty (1960) was brokered by the World Bank between India and Pakistan.

GS1.headline: Commemorating the Dandi March: A Symbol of Non-Violent Resistance and Economic Self-Reliance
GS1.explainer:
• April 6 marks the anniversary of the conclusion of Mahatma Gandhi's historic Dandi March in 1930, a pivotal moment in India's freedom struggle.
• The Salt Satyagraha challenged the British salt monopoly, symbolizing economic exploitation and colonial oppression.
• It mobilized widespread civil disobedience, drawing millions into the non-violent independence movement.
• The event underscored the power of non-violent resistance (Satyagraha) and the pursuit of Swaraj (self-rule) and economic self-sufficiency.
prelimsPoints: Dandi March began on March 12, 1930, from Sabarmati Ashram.
Concluded on April 6, 1930, at Dandi, breaking the salt law.
Led by Mahatma Gandhi with 78 initial followers.
Part of the wider Civil Disobedience Movement.
Lord Irwin was the Viceroy of India during this period.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Dandi March as a watershed moment in India's freedom struggle, highlighting its impact on mass mobilization.
Analyze how the Salt Satyagraha effectively challenged British economic policies and colonial legitimacy.
Examine the enduring relevance of Gandhian principles of non-violence and self-reliance demonstrated by the Dandi March in contemporary governance and social movements.
Evaluate the role of women and various social groups in the Civil Disobedience Movement initiated by the Dandi March.
GS1.imageDescription: Mahatma Gandhi leading the Dandi March with his followers.
GS1.trivia: The distance covered during the Dandi March was approximately 385 kilometers (240 miles) over 24 days.

GS1.headline: Roman Catholic Prelate Paschal Topno Passes Away at 92
GS1.explainer:
• Most Rev. Paschal Topno, a prominent Roman Catholic prelate, passed away at the age of 92.
• He served as the Archbishop of Ranchi, a significant diocese in India.
• His life was dedicated to spiritual guidance and social work, particularly in tribal regions of Jharkhand.
• His contributions included promoting education and healthcare in underserved communities.
prelimsPoints: Paschal Topno was the Archbishop of Ranchi.
He served the Roman Catholic Church in India.
GS1.trivia: Ranchi diocese holds historical significance for its early missionary work among tribal communities in central India.

GS1.headline: Indian Hockey Legend Raghbir Lal Breathes His Last at 95
GS1.explainer:
• Raghbir Lal, a two-time Olympic gold medalist in hockey, passed away at the age of 95.
• He was a pivotal member of the Indian hockey teams that won gold medals at the 1952 Helsinki and 1956 Melbourne Olympics.
• His demise marks the loss of a significant figure from India's golden era of hockey.
• He inspired generations of athletes with his dedication and skill on the field.
prelimsPoints: Raghbir Lal was an Indian field hockey player.
He won Olympic gold medals in 1952 (Helsinki) and 1956 (Melbourne).
India dominated field hockey during his playing career.
GS1.imageDescription: Vintage photo of Raghbir Lal playing hockey.
GS1.trivia: India won eight Olympic gold medals in men's field hockey between 1928 and 1980, a world record.

GS1.headline: Historical Significance of Sabarmati Ashram in India's Freedom Struggle
GS1.explainer:
• Sabarmati Ashram, located in Ahmedabad, served as one of Mahatma Gandhi's primary residences and a center for his activities from 1917 to 1930.
• It was from here that Gandhi launched the Dandi March on March 12, 1930, marking the beginning of the Salt Satyagraha.
• The Ashram was a laboratory for Gandhi's experiments with truth (Satyagraha), self-sufficiency (Swadeshi), and social reform.
• It remains a significant historical site, preserving Gandhi's legacy and inspiring principles of non-violence and simple living.
GS1.source: Sabarmati Ashram Preservation and Memorial Trust
prelimsPoints: Sabarmati Ashram is located on the banks of the Sabarmati River in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.
Gandhi lived here from 1917 to 1930.
It was originally known as Satyagraha Ashram.
The Dandi March began from this Ashram.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted role of Sabarmati Ashram in shaping Mahatma Gandhi's philosophy and its contribution to the Indian freedom struggle.
Analyze how the Ashram served as a model for community living and a training ground for non-violent resistance.
Examine the enduring relevance of the ideals propagated at Sabarmati Ashram in contemporary India, particularly concerning self-reliance and social justice.
GS1.imageDescription: Mahatma Gandhi's 'Hriday Kunj' at Sabarmati Ashram.
GS1.trivia: Gandhi vowed not to return to the Sabarmati Ashram until India achieved independence.

GS2.headline: Bharatiya Janata Party Marks 45th Foundation Day, Reflects on Political Journey
GS2.explainer:
• April 6, 2025, marks the 45th Foundation Day of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), established in 1980.
• The party emerged from the erstwhile Jana Sangh, which was a constituent of the Janata Party.
• Its core ideology revolves around 'Integral Humanism' and 'cultural nationalism' (Hindutva).
• Over decades, the BJP has grown from a marginal political force to the dominant party in Indian politics, leading multiple coalition and majority governments.
prelimsPoints: BJP was founded on April 6, 1980.
Its predecessor was the Bharatiya Jana Sangh, founded in 1951 by Syama Prasad Mookerjee.
Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the first President of the BJP.
The party's symbol is the lotus.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Bharatiya Janata Party since its inception, tracing its ideological shifts and electoral strategies.
Discuss the impact of 'cultural nationalism' on India's political landscape, with specific reference to the BJP's rise.
Evaluate the role of the BJP in shaping India's socio-economic policies over the past few decades.
GS2.imageDescription: BJP party symbol (Lotus) and a group of leaders.
GS2.trivia: The BJP's first major electoral breakthrough came in 1989, securing 85 seats in the Lok Sabha.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Kishna Ram Nai Passes Away at 90
GS2.explainer:
• Kishna Ram Nai, a veteran politician known for his contributions to regional politics, passed away at the age of 90.
• He served multiple terms as a legislator, advocating for the welfare of his constituents.
• His career spanned several decades, witnessing significant changes in India's political landscape.
• Nai was respected across party lines for his commitment to public service.
prelimsPoints: Kishna Ram Nai was a veteran Indian politician.
He served as a legislator for multiple terms.
GS2.imageDescription: Photo of Kishna Ram Nai addressing a gathering.
GS2.trivia: The age of eligibility for becoming a member of a State Legislative Assembly (MLA) in India is 25 years.

GS2.headline: UN Observes International Day of Sport for Development and Peace, India's Role Highlighted
GS2.explainer:
• April 6 is recognized as the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace by the United Nations.
• The day highlights the potential of sport to foster peace, promote social inclusion, and achieve sustainable development goals.
• India actively participates in global initiatives utilizing sport for youth development and community building.
• Discussions focus on leveraging sports programs for health, education, and gender equality.
prelimsPoints: International Day of Sport for Development and Peace is observed annually on April 6.
It was proclaimed by the UN General Assembly in 2013.
The day aims to recognize the positive role of sport in society.
mainsPoints: Discuss how sports can be an effective tool for achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in India, particularly in areas like health, education, and gender equality.
Analyze India's current policies and initiatives aimed at promoting sports for development and peace, and suggest areas for improvement.
Examine the role of international cooperation and UN initiatives in fostering a culture of peace and development through sports globally.
GS2.imageDescription: People participating in various sports activities, symbolizing unity and peace.
GS2.trivia: The white flag, representing peace and neutrality, has been a symbol of the Olympic movement since 1920.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act Amendments
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India is scheduled to hear multiple petitions challenging recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners raise concerns regarding potential infringements on privacy rights, government access to data, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India, balancing individual rights with the need for data processing.
• The outcome will have significant implications for data privacy, fundamental rights, and the digital economy in India.
GS2.source: Supreme Court of India / Legal News
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to regulate digital personal data.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
The Data Protection Board of India is established under the Act.
The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India and certain processing outside India.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in light of the Right to Privacy as enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
Discuss the potential impact of the DPDP Act on individual privacy, government surveillance, and the ease of doing business in the digital economy.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights in the context of evolving digital laws and technologies.
Suggest measures to strengthen data protection mechanisms while balancing national security and economic interests.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution protects the right to life and personal liberty, which the Supreme Court has interpreted to include the right to privacy.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Defence Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and defence technology.
• Discussions covered joint maritime security initiatives, counter-terrorism efforts, and strategic autonomy in a multipolar world.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, countering growing regional challenges.
• Defence collaboration, including technology transfer and co-production, remains a cornerstone of the bilateral relationship.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership.
France is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region.
Defence deals, like the Rafale fighter jets, are significant aspects of their cooperation.
Both nations advocate for a rules-based international order.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic partnership between India and France, particularly its significance in the context of Indo-Pacific geopolitics.
Discuss the various pillars of India-France cooperation, focusing on defence, space, and emerging technologies.
Evaluate how the strategic autonomy vision of both nations converges and diverges in shaping their foreign policy approaches.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India-France collaboration in addressing regional and global security threats.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Annual Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Climate Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting to discuss issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The dialogue focused on sharing data, resolving minor disputes, and the impact of climate change on water flows.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of six rivers between the two countries.
• Despite political tensions, the treaty has largely endured as a model of water-sharing cooperation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Water Resources / External Affairs
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960.
It was brokered by the World Bank.
India has control over the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).
Pakistan has control over the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
mainsPoints: Analyze the enduring significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a framework for water-sharing between India and Pakistan despite political challenges.
Discuss the potential impacts of climate change on the Indus Basin and the need for enhanced cooperation between riparian states.
Examine the role of international mediation in resolving transboundary water disputes and the lessons learned from the IWT.
Evaluate India's rights and responsibilities under the IWT concerning hydropower projects on the Western Rivers.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Green Hydrogen Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government has announced a comprehensive policy framework to boost green hydrogen production and utilization.
• The policy aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen and contribute to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key provisions include incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers, promoting R&D, and establishing green hydrogen corridors.
• This initiative is crucial for India's commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070 and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims to become a major producer and exporter of green hydrogen.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched in 2023.
Applications include fertilizers, refineries, steel, and heavy-duty transport.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the new Green Hydrogen Policy in achieving India's energy security and climate change mitigation targets.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India, including technological, economic, and infrastructural aspects.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform key industrial sectors and contribute to job creation and economic growth.
Compare India's green hydrogen strategy with global efforts and its potential to establish global leadership in this sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Evolving Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to balance controlling inflation with supporting economic growth.
• Global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility continue to pose upside risks to inflation.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while remaining watchful of growth dynamics.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rates.
The repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
The MPC consists of six members: three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate, considering both domestic and global economic factors.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting sustainable economic growth in the current macroeconomic environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in influencing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with a graph showing economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Tiger Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• India has reaffirmed its commitment to global tiger conservation, pledging increased financial and technical support.
• This comes as the latest tiger census indicates a stable to rising population in Indian reserves.
• The focus will be on combating poaching, habitat restoration, and transboundary cooperation.
• India, home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, plays a crucial role in the species' survival.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in India in 1973.
India has the largest tiger population globally.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) oversees Project Tiger.
Global Tiger Day is celebrated on July 29.
mainsPoints: Discuss the successes and challenges of India's tiger conservation efforts since the launch of Project Tiger.
Analyze the ecological and economic benefits of tiger conservation, including its role as an umbrella species.
Examine the strategies for mitigating human-wildlife conflict in areas surrounding tiger reserves.
Evaluate the importance of international cooperation in transboundary tiger conservation landscapes.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing conservation.
GS3.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals and typically hunt at night.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has conducted a successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing cost-effective and sustainable space access for India.
• The RLV program aims to reduce launch costs and enhance payload capacity by reusing rocket stages.
• India is among a few nations developing this advanced space technology, bolstering its position in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Achievements in Science and Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) is a crucial step towards future RLV.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
Other countries like the USA (SpaceX) are also developing similar technologies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its implications for global space competition.
Analyze the economic and technological advantages of RLVs compared to traditional expendable launch vehicles.
Examine the potential applications of advanced space technologies like RLVs in areas such as remote sensing, communication, and national security.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle during re-entry.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was the first interplanetary mission to be successfully launched by India.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Progress of 'PM-Gati Shakti' Master Plan for Multi-Modal Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The government conducted a high-level review of the 'PM-Gati Shakti' National Master Plan, assessing its progress in enhancing multi-modal infrastructure connectivity.
• The plan aims to integrate 16 ministries for holistic planning and synchronized implementation of infrastructure projects.
• It utilizes technology, including GIS-based mapping, to reduce logistics costs, improve efficiency, and boost economic growth.
• The review highlighted advancements in various sectors like roads, railways, ports, and digital infrastructure.
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.
It integrates 16 ministries including Railways, Roads, Ports, Shipping, Aviation, Petroleum, Power, Telecom.
The plan aims to reduce logistics costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
It uses a GIS-based platform for synchronized project planning and monitoring.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key features of the 'PM-Gati Shakti' National Master Plan and its potential to transform India's infrastructure landscape.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a large-scale, multi-ministerial integrated planning initiative and suggest ways to overcome them.
Evaluate the expected economic benefits of improved multi-modal connectivity, including reduction in logistics costs and enhanced industrial competitiveness.
Examine how PM-Gati Shakti can contribute to India's goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital map showing integrated infrastructure projects under PM-Gati Shakti.
GS3.trivia: India's logistics costs are estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than global benchmarks, which PM Gati Shakti aims to reduce.

GS3.headline: Rising Concerns Over Microplastic Pollution in Indian Rivers and Ecosystems
GS3.explainer:
• Recent studies indicate an alarming increase in microplastic pollution across major Indian rivers, posing significant environmental and health risks.
• Microplastics, tiny plastic particles, enter river systems from various sources including industrial waste, sewage, and improper plastic disposal.
• Their presence impacts aquatic life, enters the food chain, and has potential long-term effects on human health.
• Experts call for urgent policy interventions, improved waste management, and public awareness campaigns to tackle this growing crisis.
prelimsPoints: Microplastics are plastic particles less than 5mm in size.
They originate from larger plastic debris breakdown and microbeads in products.
Microplastics can carry toxins and enter the food web.
The Ganga River is one of the most studied rivers for microplastic pollution in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the sources and pathways of microplastic pollution in Indian river systems and their ecological consequences.
Discuss the potential health risks associated with microplastic contamination in water and food, and the need for comprehensive research.
Suggest a multi-pronged strategy involving policy, technology, and community participation to mitigate microplastic pollution in India.
Examine the role of a circular economy approach and improved waste management practices in addressing plastic pollution.
GS3.imageDescription: Microplastic particles visible in water, with a river in the background.
GS3.trivia: A single plastic bottle can take up to 450 years to decompose in the environment.

GS3.headline: Digital Payments Witness Sustained Growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities, Driving Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• Digital payment transactions in India continue to surge, with significant adoption observed in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities and rural areas.
• Government initiatives like UPI (Unified Payments Interface) and Jan Dhan accounts have democratized access to digital financial services.
• This growth is fostering financial inclusion, enabling easier access to credit, and boosting local economies.
• Challenges remain in terms of digital literacy, infrastructure, and cybersecurity, requiring continued policy focus.
GS3.source: National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) / Economic Survey
prelimsPoints: UPI is developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) aimed at financial inclusion.
India is a global leader in real-time digital payments.
The number of active internet users in rural areas is growing rapidly.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of digital payments, particularly UPI, in driving financial inclusion and empowering rural economies in India.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges associated with the rapid expansion of digital financial services in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Examine the government's initiatives and policy measures to promote digital payments and address issues like digital divide and cybersecurity risks.
Evaluate the potential of fintech innovations to further deepen financial inclusion and economic growth across India.
GS3.imageDescription: People using mobile phones for digital payments in a market setting.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 13.4 billion transactions worth ₹19.78 lakh crore in March 2024, reflecting its immense popularity.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Supreme Court of India / Legal News
Sabarmati Ashram Preservation and Memorial Trust
National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) / Economic Survey
Ministry of Water Resources / External Affairs
